claims.evidence_text: Wheat in Ethiopia is grown from 1500 m and 3300 m.a.s.l. ... On an average temperature of 15-25 C and an average rainfall of 400-1200 mm is required.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat requires an average temperature of 15-25 C for production.

claims.evidence_text: Suitable soil pH for wheat ranges from 5.5 to 8.0 (Garrett 1936 ).
claims.claim_summary: Suitable soil pH for wheat ranges from 5.5 to 8.0.

claims.evidence_text: It also requires 184 Nitrogen and 92 P 2 O 5 ha -1 fertilizer to get maximum yield of wheat in the surrounding areas of Ambesh watershed (Assefa et al. 2023 ).
claims.claim_summary: Wheat requires ~184 kg N/ha for maximum yield in the Ambesh watershed area (Ethiopia).

claims.evidence_text: It also requires 184 Nitrogen and 92 P 2 O 5 ha -1 fertilizer to get maximum yield of wheat in the surrounding areas of Ambesh watershed (Assefa et al. 2023 ).
claims.claim_summary: Wheat requires ~92 kg P2O5/ha for maximum yield in the Ambesh watershed area (Ethiopia).

claims.evidence_text: Wheat can be produced on different soil types such as black clay soils, red and brown soils. Sandy soils and soils with problems of waterlogging are unsuitable for wheat production.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat grows on black clay, red and brown soils; sandy soils and waterlogged soils are unsuitable.

claims.evidence_text: Sandy soils and soils with problems of waterlogging are unsuitable for wheat production.
claims.claim_summary: Soils with waterlogging problems are unsuitable for wheat production.

claims.evidence_text: It requires a high soil fertility, particularly nitrogen and phosphorus, and moderate salinity tolerance
claims.claim_summary: Wheat has moderate salinity tolerance and requires high soil fertility (especially N and P).
